findings.message: "requests" is added without any version constraint (no `>=X.Y` floor), unlike every other dependency in this manifest. An unconstrained specifier allows pip/uv to resolve arbitrarily old versions, including those with known CVEs (e.g., requests<2.31 had urllib3 header-injection issues). All other runtime dependencies carry a lower-bound pin.
findings.suggestion: Add a version floor: `"requests>=2.32"` (the current stable series). Alternatively, consider whether `httpx` (already a dev dependency) can serve the same need — it is async-native, integrates cleanly with FastAPI, and is already present in the toolchain.

findings.message: `requests` (synchronous HTTP client) is added as a production runtime dependency to a FastAPI service that already has `httpx>=0.28` in its dev group. The synchronous `requests` library blocks the event loop when called from async FastAPI handlers, and `httpx` provides an equivalent async-native API (`httpx.AsyncClient`). No justification is visible in the diff for preferring `requests` over the already-present `httpx`.
findings.suggestion: If this is for production HTTP calls from async handlers, use `httpx.AsyncClient` instead (already a declared dev dependency — promote it to runtime if needed). If `requests` is genuinely required (e.g., for a sync worker or a library that depends on it), document the reason in a comment and add the version floor.
